when_to_use: When seeking the latest research before peer review; for physics math computer science quantitative biology statistics; for cutting-edge ML/AI papers
When researching biomedical health or life sciences topics; for clinical medicine pharmacology public health; PubMed Central has full-text open access
example: pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12345678 - clinical trial on a new drug

when_to_use: When conducting literature reviews across all disciplines; for citation analysis and influential paper discovery; AI summarizes key papers
example: semanticscholar.org/paper/... - influential paper on neural networks
source_name: Semantic Scholar - Allen Institute for AI

when_to_use: When resolving DOIs to find full-text sources; for citation metadata lookup; for retrieving structured bibliographic data via API
When needing free open bibliometric data; for large-scale citation network analysis; replacement for Microsoft Academic Graph
When searching for legitimate peer-reviewed open access journals; to avoid predatory journals; for finding free full-text articles

tools: Netflix TechBlog / Uber Engineering / Meta Research
when_to_use: When researching how large-scale engineering problems are solved in production; for distributed systems ML infrastructure and case studies; high signal-to-noise
When seeking practical developer perspectives tutorials and opinion; for current trends and community discussion; verify against primary sources
example: medium.com/@author/post - tutorial on a new framework

when_to_use: When getting an initial overview of a topic or finding primary sources via references; never as a sole citation in academic work; useful for discovery not authority

tools: Mozilla MDN / React docs / Python docs
when_to_use: When looking up API syntax library usage or framework behavior; the authoritative source for how a tool actually works
When tracking real-time reactions to events breaking news or product launches; for sentiment analysis and influencer opinions; verify all claims
example: x.com/user/status/123 - announcement from a company account

when_to_use: When gauging community sentiment product feedback or niche expert discussion (e.g. r/MachineLearning); for finding edge cases and user complaints
example: reddit.com/r/MachineLearning/comments/... - discussion of a new paper

when_to_use: When troubleshooting specific technical errors; for finding canonical answers to programming questions; GitHub Issues for project-specific bugs and feature requests
example: stackoverflow.com/questions/123 - how to reverse a list in Python

when_to_use: When discovering trending tech news startups or engineering discussions; for pulse of the Silicon Valley tech community; high noise
example: news.ycombinator.com/item?id=123 - discussion of a new startup

when_to_use: When tracking current events industry news or press coverage; for finding recent announcements; evaluate each source individually
When seeking official company announcements product launches or earnings; primary source but biased toward the issuer; verify claims independently
When needing market sizing forecasts or technology adoption trends; expensive but rigorous; be aware of vendor sponsorship bias
example: gartner.com/report - Magic Quadrant for Cloud Providers

when_to_use: When analyzing competitor positioning feature sets or pricing; primary source for what a company claims about itself; inherently self-promotional
When researching startup funding history valuations investor relationships or acquisitions; for market mapping and competitive intelligence
When evaluating software tools via user reviews and ratings; for feature comparisons and real-user complaints; reviews may be incentivized or biased
